Indiatimes Why the US Banned Somali World Cup Referee Omar Artan Explained. FIFA referee Omar Artan was denied entry to the United States for the FIFA World Cup 2026 despite holding a valid visa, triggering massive international sports and immigration controversy. In this deep dive, we break down the shocking geopolitical incident that stopped Omar Artan from becoming the very first Somali official to referee a World Cup finals match. Arriving at Miami International Airport just days before the tournament kicked off, Africa’s 2025 Referee of the Year was detained and interrogated by US Customs and Border Protection (CBP) officials for 11 grueling hours. Despite presenting extensive FIFA documentation, his visa was effectively revoked, and he was placed in a holding cell before being sent back to Istanbul. We analyze the official statements from the US government citing national security and "vetting concerns," alongside the strict immigration crackdown and travel restrictions impacting over 40 nations. We also examine FIFA's neutral stance on host-government border controls and how this incident echoes wider systemic issues affecting squads like the Iranian national football team. Is international sport colliding with harsh border controls? Watch to find out.
